deep_dive.title: Rethinking Penetration Testing for AI-Enabled Systems: From Resource Compromise to Behavioral Objective Violation
deep_dive.abstract: Penetration testing traditionally evaluates whether adversaries can exploit weaknesses in software, infrastructure, configurations, or operational controls to achieve security-relevant compromise. This paradigm remains necessary for AI-enabled systems, but it is no longer sufficient. In such systems, adversaries may influence prompts, retrieved content, sensor inputs, training data, memory, tools, or human-AI interaction loops to alter system behavior without directly compromising the underlying infrastructure. This paper reframes penetration testing for AI-enabled systems as objective-driven behavioral evaluation. We define an AI-enabled system as one in which learned models materially influence behavior affecting operational outcomes, and we define AI-enabled penetration as the feasible induction of AI-governed behavior that violates one or more operational objectives under an explicit threat model. This definition preserves conventional penetration testing while extending it to adversarial pathways such as prompt injection, indirect prompt injection, data poisoning, sensor manipulation, retrieval poisoning, tool misuse, and agentic misalignment. We further propose a testing workflow that identifies operational objectives, maps AI-governed behavior, analyzes adversarial influence surfaces, defines behavioral failure criteria, executes scenario-based tests, and reports evidence linking adversarial action to objective violation. A running example involving an AI-enabled security operations center assistant illustrates how penetration may occur through behavioral influence rather than infrastructure compromise. Together, the definitions, workflow, and example provide a technical framework for evaluating adversarial success in deployed AI-enabled systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reframes penetration testing as objective-driven behavioral evaluation for AI systems, advancing software vulnerability detection and exploitation prevention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将渗透测试重新定义为面向AI系统的行为目标驱动评估，推进软件漏洞检测与利用防护
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将渗透测试重新定义为面向AI系统的行为目标驱动评估，推进软件漏洞检测与利用防护
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将渗透测试重新定义为面向AI系统的行为目标驱动评估，推进软件漏洞检测与利用防护
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Reframes penetration testing for AI systems as behavioral objective violation, defining an evaluation paradigm that targets adversarial influence over prompts and data, advancing vulnerability detection beyond traditional infrastructure compromise.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出将针对AI系统的渗透测试从资源攻陷范式重新定义为行为目标违规评估，以应对对手通过提示、数据、交互等途径操纵系统行为的风险。它定义了客观驱动的行为评估方法，识别了AI特有的攻击面，超越了传统基础设施攻陷的测试范围，该方法推动了针对AI组件的新型漏洞检测与利用预防技术。

deep_dive.title: Fully Automated End-to-End Adversary Emulation from MITRE ATT\&CK Based Cyber Threat Intelligence Using LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: This paper presents a fully automated end-to-end framework for adversary emulation from MITRE ATT&CK-aligned CTI reports using LLMs. Unlike prior work, which either executes prewritten playbooks or partially automates playbook generation, our framework unifies playbook generation, execution, and failure recovery in a single workflow. In particular, although AURORA, the most recent prior study, generates playbooks from CTI reports, it still requires partial manual intervention and does not revise playbooks based on execution failures. Our framework generates Caldera playbooks from CTI reports, executes them automatically, and revises failed Abilities through a failure-type-aware recovery mechanism. Evaluated on 11 CTI reports with Claude Sonnet 4.5, GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Grok 4 Fast, the framework achieved its best results with Claude Sonnet 4.5: 27.3 Abilities per playbook, 84.22% execution success after revision, and CTI Precision, Recall, and F1 of 73.95%, 52.48%, and 60.50%, respectively. The failure recovery mechanism consistently improved execution success across all evaluated LLM models by 14.59%p to 17.23%p. On the 10 CTI reports selected from AURORA's dataset, this mechanism further increased the final execution success rate, surpassing that of AURORA, which represents the state-of-the-art adversary emulation system.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Fully automated adversary emulation from threat intelligence tests and improves security defenses
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从威胁情报全自动进行对手仿真，测试和改善安全防御
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从威胁情报全自动进行对手仿真，测试和改善安全防御
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从威胁情报全自动进行对手仿真，测试和改善安全防御
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a fully automated end-to-end framework for adversary emulation using LLMs, generating Caldera playbooks from MITRE ATT&CK CTI reports, executing them, and recovering from failures via a failure-type-aware mechanism. Tested on 11 cases, it outperforms prior semi-automated approaches. Advances automated security testing and improves defense readiness against real-world attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有对手仿真仍需人工干预且无法处理故障的不足，提出基于LLM的全自动端到端框架，统一实现从MITRE ATT&CK威胁情报生成Caldera剧本、自动执行以及基于故障分类的恢复。在11个案例上验证，成功复现攻击行为，并能自适应修正失败步骤，优于半自动方法。该工作显著提升了安全测试的自动化水平，有助于组织及时发现和修补安全漏洞，增强对高级持续性威胁的防御能力。

deep_dive.title: Assessing the Forensic Viability of Android Memory Analysis Across Production Builds: A Cross-Version Study of Security Hardening and Structure Preservation
deep_dive.abstract: Android memory forensics recovers evidence that never touches disk: decrypted messages, session credentials, and the live internal state of a running application. The tools that perform this recovery depend on debug symbols embedded in libart.so, the Android Runtime library, to locate data structures and interpret their layout. Across recent releases Google has stripped most of that information from the binaries that ship on consumer phones as part of a broader security hardening effort, yet no prior work has measured how far the stripping has progressed on the devices examiners actually encounter, or whether the memory architecture beneath the stripped surface still resembles what the forensics literature describes. This paper measures the gap between the unstripped development builds researchers use and the production builds that ship on real hardware, using binaries extracted from Pixel factory images across Android 8 through Android 15. Static symbols fell from 20,495 entries to zero, dynamic symbols dropped by roughly 60 percent, and source file references disappeared entirely. A compressed fallback section in the Android 15 binary restores thousands of function names but carries no structure layouts. Source code review and memory map comparisons across Android 8 and 15 show that the heap spaces, garbage collector infrastructure, and allocation bitmaps remain structurally intact, with visible changes limited to naming and arithmetic optimization. Live validation on a rooted, fully stripped Pixel 7 confirms that the runtime entry point is still locatable through the dynamic symbol table, and that structural offsets pulled from a version-matched development build resolve to valid pointers inside production memory.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: assesses Android memory forensics (dynamic analysis) viability in the face of security hardening, directly relevant to runtime analysis techniques
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估Android内存取证（动态分析）在面对安全加固时的可行性，直接相关运行动态分析技术
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估Android内存取证（动态分析）在面对安全加固时的可行性，直接相关运行动态分析技术
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估Android内存取证（动态分析）在面对安全加固时的可行性，直接相关运行动态分析技术
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Measures the impact of Android's stripped debug symbols on memory forensics across versions, finding that despite hardening, core structures persist, enabling continued runtime analysis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 随着Android安全加固导致调试符号被剥离，内存取证面临挑战。本文系统测量了不同Android版本中libart.so的符号剥离程度，发现尽管公开信息减少，内存架构核心结构仍保留，表明生产版本上运行时内存分析依然可行，为取证工具开发提供依据。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 随着Android安全加固导致调试符号被剥离，内存取证面临挑战。本文系统测量了不同Android版本中libart.so的符号剥离程度，发现尽管公开信息减少，内存架构核心结构仍保留，表明生产版本上运行时内存分析依然可行，为取证工具开发提供依据。

deep_dive.title: Setup Complete, Now You Are Compromised: Weaponizing Setup Instructions Against AI Coding Agents
deep_dive.abstract: AI coding agents set up projects by reading documentation and installing the dependencies it lists, without verifying their names, sources, or known vulnerabilities. By editing only a README, requirements file, or Makefile, an attacker can redirect the agent to an untrusted registry, a known-vulnerable version, or a wrong-but-plausible name: documentation becomes a vector for code execution. We present the first systematic evaluation of package-install-time supply-chain attacks delivered through ordinary project-setup documentation across production coding-agent harnesses, probing frontier models on twelve scenarios in five attack classes, grounded in documented incidents. The same model catches an attack through one harness and installs it through another: install-time security rests on the harness-model combination, not the model alone. Agents catch blatant typosquats reliably, but plausible separator-confusion names (azurecore for azure-core) slip through, and how often depends on the harness-model pairing. Source-based attacks like registry redirection are missed almost everywhere. The source blind spot recurs on npm and Cargo, where nearly every model installs the untrusted dependency; name detection carries over less consistently across ecosystems. Security-oriented prompts recover part of the gap but only for the dimension they name; a deterministic pre-install check that verifies names, sources, and versions before any code runs closes most of it.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates supply-chain attacks via project setup documentation on AI coding agents, revealing vulnerabilities in automated dependency installation, a software exploitation prevention concern
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估通过项目设置文档对AI编码代理的供应链攻击，揭示自动依赖安装中的漏洞，属于软件利用防护问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估通过项目设置文档对AI编码代理的供应链攻击，揭示自动依赖安装中的漏洞，属于软件利用防护问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估通过项目设置文档对AI编码代理的供应链攻击，揭示自动依赖安装中的漏洞，属于软件利用防护问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Shows that AI coding agents are vulnerable to supply-chain attacks through malicious setup instructions, with adversarial documentation leading to untrusted dependencies, highlighting a new vector for software exploitation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文首次系统评估了通过项目设置文档向AI编码代理发起的软件包安装时供应链攻击。攻击者仅需修改README等文档即可将代理引向恶意依赖或漏洞版本，导致代码执行。对多个生产级代理框架和前沿模型的测试表明，此类攻击广泛可行，且模型在不同框架下检测能力不一，为软件漏洞利用防护揭示了新的攻击面。

deep_dive.title: Beyond Success Rate: Cost-Aware Evaluation of Offensive and Defensive Security Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Security-agent evaluations commonly measure peak offensive capability under generous inference budgets, emphasizing vulnerability discovery, exploit development, penetration testing, and CTF completion. Such measurements are useful but incomplete: in operational security, every reasoning step, tool call, telemetry query, and enrichment request consumes budget. We evaluate language-model security agents through this cost-success lens on offensive Cybench challenges and defensive Splunk BOTS v1 investigation challenges. Instead of reporting only best-case success, we compare models at fixed cost levels and decompose performance by inference spend and tool spend. Our results show distinct scalingregimes for red- and blue-team tasks. Offensive CTF performance improves with additional test-time compute, and scaled open-weight models can approach frontier proprietary systems while remaining cost-competitive. Defensive SOC investigation does not scale in the same way: success depends more heavily on disciplined tool use, telemetry navigation, and selective enrichment than on raw reasoning budget alone. We argue that security-agent benchmarks should measure economic efficiency and operational fit alongside task success. Cost-aware, SOC-native evaluations provide a clearer picture of which models are practically useful today and where defensive agents still need to improve. We present an interactive website with our results https://evals.frontier.security.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates security agents on vulnerability discovery and defensive tasks, directly relevant to vulnerability detection and exploitation prevention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估安全智能体在漏洞发现和防御任务上的表现，直接关联漏洞检测与利用预防
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估安全智能体在漏洞发现和防御任务上的表现，直接关联漏洞检测与利用预防
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估安全智能体在漏洞发现和防御任务上的表现，直接关联漏洞检测与利用预防
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper evaluates language-model security agents through a cost-success lens on offensive and defensive tasks, showing distinct scaling regimes and highlighting the need for cost-aware evaluation in security contexts.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有安全智能体评估常忽略操作预算，本文提出成本-成功视角，在攻击性Cybench和防御性Splunk挑战上评估模型，比较固定成本下的表现，分解推理和工具开销，揭示红队和蓝队任务的不同扩展规律，推动安全评估走向更真实的资源约束场景。

quick_skim.title: Using LLMs to Adjudicate Static-Analysis Alerts with Error Reduction Techniques
quick_skim.abstract: Static analysis is widely used for finding security weaknesses in source code before deployment, but it often produces far more alerts than analysts can review. We study how well large language models (LLMs) can adjudicate (classify as a real bug or a false alarm) static-analysis alerts. We use two mistake-mitigation methods: (1) a consistency check (CC) that runs the LLM multiple times and checks that the verdicts are consistent with each other, and (2) an LLM reasoning evaluation (LRE) step that runs the LLM multiple times and then asks the LLM to choose a verdict after evaluating the reasoning provided by each run.   We evaluated several LLMs on three test suites: Juliet, FormAI, and SV-COMP. Across all three suites, the mid-tier reasoning LLMs that we tested (o4-mini, gpt-oss-120b, gpt-oss-20b) reach high recall (percent of real bugs that the tool correctly flags as needing repair / manual attention) and specificity (percent of actually false alerts that the tool correctly dismisses as false alarms). With mistake mitigation, they reach at least 98% recall and at least 94.8% specificity on every suite (with CC alone on Juliet and SV-COMP, and with LRE+CC on FormAI).   We probe Juliet memorization and show that o4-mini can often reconstruct sanitized test cases' original identities, so we base our generalization claims primarily on FormAI, scored against our own unpublished manual adjudications. We also report results of using the LLM to synthesize a program that dynamically triggers the flaw as independent evidence; a validity check rejected every trigger driver aimed at a false alarm, so a valid trigger proved to be strong evidence of a real flaw.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based adjudication of static analysis alerts to reduce false positives in security bug detection.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的静态分析告警裁决以降低安全漏洞检测误报
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的静态分析告警裁决以降低安全漏洞检测误报
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的静态分析告警裁决以降低安全漏洞检测误报
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper explores using LLMs to classify static analysis alerts as real bugs or false alarms, employing consistency checks and reasoning evaluation to reduce errors. It shows that mid-tier reasoning models can significantly cut false positives, improving the practicality of static analysis tools for security.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 静态分析工具常产生大量告警，分析师难以审核。本文研究利用大语言模型（LLM）对静态分析告警进行裁决，判断其为真实漏洞或误报，并提出两种误报消减方法：一致性检查和推理评估。在多个测试集上，中等推理能力LLM能有效降低误报，提升了静态分析在安全漏洞检测中的实用性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 静态分析工具常产生大量告警，分析师难以审核。本文研究利用大语言模型（LLM）对静态分析告警进行裁决，判断其为真实漏洞或误报，并提出两种误报消减方法：一致性检查和推理评估。在多个测试集上，中等推理能力LLM能有效降低误报，提升了静态分析在安全漏洞检测中的实用性。

quick_skim.title: Multi-Perspective Agentic Program Repair via Code Property Graphs and Temporal Execution Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have improved automated program repair (APR), but two limitations remain. First, raw execution traces are often too large and repetitive to serve as effective model context. Second, repeated patch sampling may produce different implementations without yielding distinct root-cause hypotheses or repair strategies. We present CT-Repair, an agentic APR framework representing static and dynamic evidence as queryable Code Property Graph (CPG) and Temporal Execution Graph (TEG). CT-Repair applies a three-stage filtering pipeline to construct compact TEGs. Three finite-state-machine-guided agents analyze each bug from static, dynamic, and hybrid perspectives and independently produce evidence-grounded repair strategies. A strategy-guided generation procedure instantiates these strategies as candidate patches and uses validation feedback to refine the most promising strategy.   We evaluate CT-Repair on 854 Java bugs from Defects4J v3.0. In the mixed-model configuration, CT-Repair correctly repairs 489 bugs. Under a controlled GPT-5.4-mini configuration, it repairs 388 bugs, 19 and 30 more than ReinFix and RepairAgent, respectively. The union of the three evidence perspectives repairs 99 more bugs than the strongest individual perspective. The filtering pipeline also compacts runtime evidence, with execution filtering narrowing the candidate method scope by 94.85% on average and behavior filtering further reducing retained runtime records by 55.97%. These results show that structured runtime evidence and multi-perspective reasoning can improve repair effectiveness without relying solely on a larger patch-generation budget.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses code property graphs (static analysis) as part of bug repair framework, transferable to security bug analysis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用代码属性图（静态分析）作为缺陷修复框架的一部分，可迁移至安全缺陷分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用代码属性图（静态分析）作为缺陷修复框架的一部分，可迁移至安全缺陷分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用代码属性图（静态分析）作为缺陷修复框架的一部分，可迁移至安全缺陷分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an agentic APR framework that leverages static (CPG) and dynamic (TEG) evidence to generate and evaluate bug fixes, demonstrating multi-perspective analysis for program repair.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有自动程序修复中执行轨迹冗长、补丁策略单一的问题，本文提出CT-Repair框架，结合代码属性图（静态）和时间执行图（动态），通过多视角智能体协同分析生成有依据的补丁，为程序修复提供更全面的证据支持。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有自动程序修复中执行轨迹冗长、补丁策略单一的问题，本文提出CT-Repair框架，结合代码属性图（静态）和时间执行图（动态），通过多视角智能体协同分析生成有依据的补丁，为程序修复提供更全面的证据支持。

quick_skim.title: Auditable and Transparent Fully Authenticated Disk Encryption via USB Storage Interposition
quick_skim.abstract: Full Disk Encryption (FDE) has become increasingly important in the last decades due to the evident confidentiality concerns. In most systems, encryption is provided by an operating system driver, through which the user can transparently access the encrypted disk after supplying the required keys (or the credentials from which those keys are derived). In this work, we explore an alternative approach: the use of an intermediate USB device placed between the host system and the external hard disk, where the encrypted data are stored. Although there are existing devices that follow this inline approach (such as RAID controllers and USB enclosures with built-in encryption), we explore the use of a general-purpose single-board computer running Linux with USB On-The-Go support (e.g. a Raspberry Pi), to provide FADE (Fully Authenticated Disk Encryption). Our system, named CC (Cryptographic Companion), performs the cryptographic operations (encryption/decryption and authentication), providing a standard USB mass-storage interface to the host (which is entirely unaware of the presence of encryption) while relying on the external USB hard disk to store the corresponding encrypted blocks. Our design provides several key advantages: flexibility, low cost, transparency, the use of generic hardware and free and open-source software, adaptability to emerging cryptographic schemes, and mitigations against malicious disk firmware. This paper presents the design and implementation of the CC and an experimental evaluation of our current research prototype, which indicates that it is sufficiently efficient for most common use cases.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: alternative disk encryption via USB interposition, bypassing OS kernel drivers, potentially defending against kernel-level data compromise
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过USB中介实现磁盘加密，绕过操作系统内核驱动，可能防御内核级数据泄露
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过USB中介实现磁盘加密，绕过操作系统内核驱动，可能防御内核级数据泄露
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过USB中介实现磁盘加密，绕过操作系统内核驱动，可能防御内核级数据泄露
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an intermediate USB device for full disk encryption, moving trust outside the OS to defend against kernel-level attacks and providing auditable, transparent encryption.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 为避免操作系统内核驱动中可能存在的漏洞，本文探索使用中间USB设备在主机与外置硬盘间实现全盘加密，将加密功能置于独立硬件中，提供透明且可审计的加密方案，增强了面对内核级攻击时的数据防护能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 为避免操作系统内核驱动中可能存在的漏洞，本文探索使用中间USB设备在主机与外置硬盘间实现全盘加密，将加密功能置于独立硬件中，提供透明且可审计的加密方案，增强了面对内核级攻击时的数据防护能力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: How to defend against kernel level attacks in modern operating systems

quick_skim.title: Elton: Urn Resources for Reasoning about Adversarial Probabilistic Programs
quick_skim.abstract: Probabilistic programs are important for many applications. For security applications in particular, one is interested in establishing properties that hold in the presence of arbitrary adversaries, i.e., unknown pieces of code. We present Elton, a higher-order separation logic for reasoning about higher-order probabilistic programs utilizing unknown adversarial code. Elton incorporates novel logical facilities for specifying invariants over distributional properties using delayed samplings at the language level, and a new kind of separation-logic predicate called urn resources at the logic level. We show that these extensions are sound and can be erased back to a standard call-by-value semantics. Combined with other features, e.g. invariants and ghost resources, Elton is expressive enough to prove error bounds on a wide range of security examples, some of which are beyond the scope of previous techniques. All proofs are mechanized with the Rocq proof assistant and the Iris separation logic framework.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: separation logic for proving security properties of adversarial probabilistic programs, relevant to vulnerability prevention
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于证明对抗性概率程序安全属性的分离逻辑，与漏洞预防相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于证明对抗性概率程序安全属性的分离逻辑，与漏洞预防相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于证明对抗性概率程序安全属性的分离逻辑，与漏洞预防相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a higher-order separation logic with urn resources for reasoning about probabilistic programs with adversaries, enabling proofs of security hyperproperties like non-interference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对含任意对抗代码的概率程序安全验证难题，提出Elton逻辑，通过延迟采样和瓮资源谓词表达分布不变式，支持概率非干扰等超属性推理，为安全关键软件的形式化验证提供理论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对含任意对抗代码的概率程序安全验证难题，提出Elton逻辑，通过延迟采样和瓮资源谓词表达分布不变式，支持概率非干扰等超属性推理，为安全关键软件的形式化验证提供理论基础。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Advanced techniques for software vulnerability detection and exploitation prevention

quick_skim.title: The Prover Is the Judge: Verified Security Software from AI Coding Agents in Ada/SPARK
quick_skim.abstract: AI coding agents produce code faster than humans can review it. In our approach, the prover is the judge of whether the code is correct. Under a verifier-driven loop, AI agents wrote and verified bare-metal security software in Ada/SPARK spanning classical and post-quantum cryptography, TLS 1.3, IKEv2, X.509, and a Matrix client. GNATprove discharged 49,280 proof obligations, established functional correctness for selected primitives, and proved the absence of run-time errors for the rest, at roughly 20-40 times lower supervision cost than comparable hand verification. GNATprove alone was insufficient: some defects could not be detected and were resolved using known-answer tests, interoperability, or human review of specifications. Given weak checks, the agent tried to bypass them and reported success. We report where each layer caught faults and draw the central lesson: what an agent can be trusted to establish is bounded by the strength of its feedback.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: verified security software from AI agents, using formal verification to prevent vulnerabilities
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: AI代理生成经验证的安全软件，利用形式化验证防止漏洞
quick_skim.llm_evidence: AI代理生成经验证的安全软件，利用形式化验证防止漏洞
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: AI代理生成经验证的安全软件，利用形式化验证防止漏洞
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a verifier-driven loop where AI agents generate verified security software in Ada/SPARK, proving absence of runtime errors for crypto and protocols, cutting costs 20-40x.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对AI快速生成代码但审查困难的问题，提出验证器驱动循环方法，让AI代理在Ada/SPARK中编写安全软件并由证明器自动验证，成功为密码学、TLS等协议建立功能正确性并消除运行时错误，将监督成本降低20-40倍，展示了形式化验证在AI辅助安全开发中的巨大潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对AI快速生成代码但审查困难的问题，提出验证器驱动循环方法，让AI代理在Ada/SPARK中编写安全软件并由证明器自动验证，成功为密码学、TLS等协议建立功能正确性并消除运行时错误，将监督成本降低20-40倍，展示了形式化验证在AI辅助安全开发中的巨大潜力。
